HomePage.title: Start from details, then explore the world.
HomePage.description: Random showcases present slices of Austrum, while latest champions and countries stay pinned as curated entrances.

AboutPage.title: About Austrum
AboutPage.intro: Austrum is an explorable worldbuilding archive where characters, regions, creatures, and stories connect into a living network.
AboutPage.exploreDesc: Enter from visual gateways and follow internal links to roam through connected entries.
AboutPage.structureDesc: Countries, regions, creatures, champions and stories reference each other as a network rather than a strict hierarchy.

ContactPage.title: Contact us
ContactPage.description: Share suggestions, collaboration ideas, or worldbuilding additions here.
ContactPage.name: Name
ContactPage.success: Submitted successfully. We will reply soon.
ContactPage.error: Submission failed. Please try again later.
ContactPage.notesBody: If you have character concepts or worldbuilding material for Austrum, feel free to contact us.
ContactPage.tipsBody: Please include a clear topic and context so we can integrate your idea efficiently.

CountriesPage.title: Countries & Organizations
CountriesPage.lead: Enter the world through political entities and organizations.
CountriesPage.empty: No countries or organizations yet. Create entries in Studio first.

RegionsPage.title: Region Gallery
RegionsPage.lead: Use maps and environments as clues to enter each fragment of the world.
RegionsPage.empty: No region entries yet. Create entries in Studio first.

StoriesPage.title: Story Gallery
StoriesPage.lead: Connect emotions and clues of the world through stories.
StoriesPage.empty: No stories yet. Create entries in Studio first.
StoriesPage.defaultSummary: Enter a chapter and follow clues to other world entries.

ChampionsPage.title: Champion Gallery
ChampionsPage.lead: Every champion is an exhibit. Pick one and enter their legend.
ChampionsPage.empty: No champion entries yet. Create entries in Studio first.

ChampionsFilter.empty: No champions match the selected filters. Try clearing filters.

CreaturesPage.title: Creature Gallery
CreaturesPage.lead: Catalog species and ecology slices across the world.
CreaturesPage.empty: No creature entries yet. Create entries in Studio first.

CreaturesFilter.empty: No creatures match the selected filters.

MagicsPage.title: Magic Atlas
MagicsPage.lead: Split magic into principles and spells to understand world rules and practical usage.
MagicsPage.empty: No magic entries yet. Create entries in Studio first.

MagicsFilter.summaryFallback: Open this magic entry to view full details and linked content.

HeroDetailPage.quoteFallback: If I do not even know the rules, how can breaking them define me?
HeroDetailPage.bioEmpty: Biography content will be added later.

StoryDetailPage.emptyContent: Story body content will be added in later iterations with richer layout and narrative structure.

FullscreenHero.welcomeSubtitle: A worldbuilding archive entered through details

Overview.title: Shape concepts in chat, settle them into the Entry Card, then decide when to push to Sanity
Overview.description: Writer now supports two entry paths: start from scratch with AI, or import an existing entry for continuation, rewriting, and calibration. Either way, you finish in the same dual-pane workspace.
Overview.howItWorksStep1: Choose either "Conversation creation" or "Edit existing entry" in `/writer/new`.
Overview.howItWorksStep2: Enter with the right provider and presets so AI starts in the correct direction from round one.
Overview.howItWorksStep3: Keep discussing in chat while the Entry Card stays synchronized.
Overview.statsWorkflowValue: Chat collaboration / Entry Card editing / checks and Sanity push
Overview.recentDescription: Continue an existing session, or delete temporary drafts you no longer need.
Overview.emptySessions: No drafts yet. Create a new session, or import an existing entry to begin editing.
Overview.deleteConfirm: Delete draft "{title}"? This cannot be undone.

conversation.label: Conversation creation
conversation.description: Start from a concept, discuss with AI first, converge it into the Entry Card, then validate and push to Sanity.

steps.one: Describe the concept and starting assumptions
steps.two: Let AI chat and keep the card in sync

import.label: Edit existing entry
import.description: Import an existing Sanity entry with its fields and body into Writer. Best for continuation, rewriting, and calibration.

steps.one: Locate an existing entry by type
steps.three: Discuss and update the card before pushing again

NewEntry.heroTitle: Start from a concept, or import an existing entry into the current chat-driven writing workflow
NewEntry.heroDescription: Writer now supports two better entry points: discuss a setting from scratch with AI, or bring an existing entry in for continuation, rewriting, and calibration. Both paths land in the same Entry Card + Chat workspace.

summary.step1Description: Decide whether to begin from a concept or continue from an existing entry.
summary.step2Description: Enable worldview, style, and rule presets before the session starts so AI understands the right direction immediately.
summary.step3Description: AI advances the discussion in chat while synchronizing confirmed content to the Entry Card, and you decide when to push.

NewEntry.workspaceDescription: The two entry paths here are "Conversation creation" from scratch and "Edit existing entry" by bringing a Sanity entry into the current workspace.
NewEntry.workspaceBadge: Entry Card + Chat is the only workspace

fields.titlePlaceholder: Examples: Tidal Alliance, Ashfrost Wastes, Nightcall Caravan
fields.titleHint: This is only a starting title. You can keep adjusting it later in chat and on the card.
fields.sourcePlaceholder: Write what you already know, for example: this is a coastal nation built on tidal religion; I want it to feel both mercantile and ritualistic; I still haven't decided how it relates to the northern frontier, or whether it should be a country or a region.
fields.sourceHint: Recommended structure: core concept, desired vibe, known facts, and the questions you still haven't settled. AI will use this to open the first round of discussion.
fields.documentTypeHint: If you're unsure, let AI classify it first. You can still change it later.
fields.providerHint: Choose either a local CLI or a configured API provider here.
fields.importDescription: Choose an entry type and bring an existing item into Writer. After the session starts, you can still keep rewriting, extending, and calibrating it through chat.

presets.title: Starting presets
presets.description: These presets take effect as soon as the session is created and influence the first chat round, generation, outlining, and later calibration.
presets.empty: No presets are currently available. You can add worldview, style, or calibration presets later in `/writer/settings`.

buttons.classify: Let AI classify the entry type

preview.title: This session will revolve around the Entry Card
preview.description: After entering `/writer/[sessionId]`, the Entry Card sits on the left and chat on the right. Each AI reply will try to keep the card synchronized while respecting the fields you lock manually.
preview.noteConversation: What you fill in now is only the starting point, not the final draft. Type, fields, text, and push strategy can all keep changing later.
preview.noteImport: Importing does not freeze the structure. Once inside Writer, you can still restructure, extend body text, adjust fields, and push again from chat.

aiSuggestions.title: AI type suggestions
aiSuggestions.description: If the concept is still fuzzy, let AI judge whether it feels closer to a Country, Region, Creature, or another type.
aiSuggestions.empty: After clicking "Let AI classify the entry type", candidate types and reasoning will appear here.

importInfo.title: What happens when you edit an existing entry
importInfo.item1: Importing brings the current Sanity fields and values into Writer instead of giving you an empty draft.
importInfo.item2: After entering the session, you can still ask AI in chat to summarize gaps, expand the body, unify tone, and check worldview conflicts.
importInfo.item3: If presets are enabled, AI will use those worldview and writing requirements from the very first round.

SettingsPage.title: Manage model providers and writing presets
SettingsPage.description: Use this page to maintain local CLI/API providers plus presets for worldview, writing requirements, and task rules. Changes take effect immediately in `/writer/new` and active Writer sessions.
SettingsPage.tip1: Keep clear names for frequently used models so they are easy to switch in a session.
SettingsPage.tip2: Split worldview rules and writing style into composable presets instead of stuffing everything into one giant prompt.
SettingsPage.tip3: Pick the provider and presets before creating a session so AI starts in the right direction from the first round.

ProviderManager.baseUrlPlaceholder: Base URL, leave blank to use the default
ProviderManager.apiKeyPlaceholderEdit: Fill only if you want to replace the API key; leave blank to keep the current one

PresetManager.contentPlaceholder: Write worldview rules, writing requirements, forbidden settings, output style, or task constraints...

ExistingEntryPicker.searchPlaceholder: Search by title, slug, or summary

FieldInput.stringArrayPlaceholder: One item per line, or separate with commas
FieldInput.imagePlaceholder: Enter a Sanity asset reference ID (optional)

PortableText.placeholder: Write in natural paragraphs. Writer will convert this to Portable Text automatically when submitting.

hints.summary: Recommended 50-120 characters for a concise overview of the entry.
hints.bio: Recommended 200-500 characters, split into 2-4 paragraphs for readability.
hints.content: Recommended 1000+ characters, ideally split into multiple natural paragraphs.
hints.details: Recommended to explain rules, origin, risks, and effects in separate paragraphs.
hints.default: Recommended to write in natural paragraphs so it converts cleanly into Portable Text later.

ReferenceFieldPicker.empty: Results will appear after you enter keywords.

StructuredFieldEditor.groupDescription: Structured fields generated automatically from the current Sanity schema.

ConversationTimeline.emptyTitle: Start by talking through the concept
ConversationTimeline.emptyDescription: Tell AI about the concept, tone, gaps, or conflicts. It will refine the setting through conversation and keep the Entry Card synchronized.
ConversationTimeline.placeholder: Continue the discussion, or tell AI how to change the Entry Card. Press Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line.
ConversationTimeline.footerNote: Messages appear instantly and the full chat history is preserved.

EntryCard.allRequiredPresent: All required fields are present. You can run checks, calibrate, or submit to Sanity.

Workbench.deleteConfirm: Delete draft "{title}"? This cannot be undone.
Workbench.generationUpdatedFields: AI updated the card and wrote {count} fields.
Workbench.optimisticThinking: AI is thinking and syncing the entry card...
Workbench.messageSendFailed: Message send failed because the session could not be saved.
Workbench.chatSyncedFields: AI replied and synchronized {count} card fields.
Workbench.chatUpdatedUnderstanding: AI replied and updated the current understanding.
Workbench.chatNetworkInline: Network or service error. This message was not submitted successfully.
Workbench.promptPresetsDescription: Let presets handle exploration, convergence, and conflict checks while keeping the chat natural.
